intro_zh: 先听完整课文，再把坏消息预期、紧张语气和最后反转读出来。

sentences.text: The secretary told me that Mr. Harmsworth would see me.
sentences.focus_zh: told me that 是转述；would see me 表示老板要见我，读出消息突然来的感觉。

sentences.text: I felt very nervous when I went into his office.
sentences.focus_zh: very nervous 要读出紧张和不确定，像被严肃地叫去谈话。

sentences.text: He did not look up from his desk when I entered.
sentences.focus_zh: did not look up 是气氛变紧的动作，读得安静一点。

sentences.text: He told me that the firm could not afford to pay such large salaries.
sentences.focus_zh: firm 是公司；could not afford 是付不起，读出坏消息线索。
had already left 表示已经离开，给 my turn had come 做铺垫。

sentences.text: I knew that my turn had come.
sentences.focus_zh: my turn had come 在这里是“坏事轮到我了”，语气要担心。
Don't interrupt 是短而硬的一句，读出老板打断他的压力。

sentences.text: Then he smiled and told me I would receive an extra thousand pounds a year!
sentences.focus_zh: smiled 后语气转暖；extra thousand pounds a year 是最后的好消息。
